summary: 2026-07-16 10:31 PDT — The change largely implements the approved consult-presentation design, and the targeted reviewer/dev-audit linters pass. One specification mismatch remains. The full configured gate could not run in the read-only sandbox because guard tests require temporary-directory writes.

findings.title: Recommendation grounding names the workflow instead of the app
findings.claim: AC1 explicitly requires recommendations to be groundable in “the purpose of the app,” but the new doctrine says “the purpose of the workflow.” These are not interchangeable: the workflow reviews other applications, and their product purpose may determine the appropriate disposition. The changed wording removes an approved grounding source and can produce recommendations based on the review machinery rather than the product under review.
findings.suggestion: Replace “the purpose of the workflow” with “the purpose of the app” (or “the purpose of the app under review”) to match AC1 and the approved design sketch.
